text:
There was once a tiny village nestled at the heart of a sprawling forest. The village consisted of a few sturdy cottages and an ancient oak tree at its center. On warm afternoons, villagers would gather under the oak to enjoy lemon cakes and tales of valor.

One fateful autumn day, as the leaves began to fall, a strange noise echoed through the valley. Villagers whispered and glanced around, their eyes wide with suspicion — was it the wind? No.

A giant sequoia tree had fallen, its weight carving a gap through the forest floor. The villagers knew the tree was old, but they had never imagined its fall would shake their entire world.

For days, the village bore witness to this awe-inspiring sight. Children imagined that the giant sequoia's shadow could sweep over the land and cover them in mystical leaves. Adults pondered what secrets the tree held and what kind of creature had pushed it over.

No sooner had the shock faded than a strange figure appeared
